subject: Gaming
title: The Zeigarnik Effect in Games
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Zeigarnik Effect in Games.

hook.text: You can't stop thinking about an unfinished quest. That nagging feeling has a name: the Zeigarnik effect.

buildup.text: Psychologist Bluma Zeigarnik found that people remember incomplete tasks better than completed ones.

discovery.text: Games exploit this by giving you multiple unfinished objectives. Your brain demands completion.

twist.text: Open-world games overwhelm you with question marks on purpose. Each one creates a mental itch.

climax.text: The Zeigarnik effect is why you say 'one more quest' and play for three more hours.

punchline.text: Unfinished quests haunt your brain. One more quest becomes three more hours.

quiz.question: What is the Zeigarnik effect in gaming?
options: The tendency to remember and feel compelled to complete unfinished tasks
